title: Why Devon Energy Is A Steal Right Now
text: This article starts with a discussion of my bullish outlook on oil and gas prices, driven by high demand and subdued supply growth, leading to a widening supply gap. In order to capitalize on this, I prefer Devon Energy, which comes with high oil and gas volumes, efficient operations, low debt levels, and a focus on shareholder distributions.

title: Is Devon Energy Stock A Buy On The Dip? Yes, Concerns Are Overdone
text: Devon Energy's shares have underperformed recently due to investor worries about the company's capital expenditures and production. My opinion is that investor concerns regarding DVN's 2023 production and capital expenditures are overblown, and I expect Devon Energy's share price to recover in due course.

title: Devon Energy: The Pain Is Not Over
text: Shares have fallen over 15% since earnings on February 14th due to higher CAPEX expenses and lower commodity prices. Production issues and capital spending will reduce free cash flow for the 1st half of 2023.

title: Devon Energy: Market Overreactions Can Lead To Big Gains - And Dividends
text: A variable dividend can be a nice feature for income seeking investors in good times (i.e. high energy prices), but can make it difficult to predict actual returns. Due to Devon's policy to payout approximately 90 days after quarter end, I will discuss a correlation to predict upcoming quarter payouts and why $57/share is an attractive entry point.

title: Devon Energy: Risks To The Dividend, But This Is Still Attractive
text: Devon Energy cut back its variable dividend in Q3. I lay out an investment case where this variable dividend gets further reduced in 2023. According to my estimates, if Devon's total variable dividend comes out at $5 per share in 2023, investors would still get an 8.2% total yield.
